title: Karnataka notifies reservation for 369 wards of new Bengaluru corporations, questions persist over quotas
content: However, the final notification is likely to be challenged in the court, as the Urban Development Department has not changed the percentage in either vertical reservation or the women quota, despite the draft notification drawing widespread criticism over the same.

title: Karnataka High Court judge injured in convoy accident on Bengaluru–Tumakuru Highway
content: According to the police, the incident occurred at around 7.30 a.m. when a truck travelling ahead of the judge’s convoy suddenly took a left turn.

title: ‘Each One Teach One’ model launched in Mandya to improve SSLC results
content: ZP CEO Nandini K.R. said while about 68% students had passed in Preparatory Examination-1, the pass percentage increased to 86 per cent in Preparatory Examination-3. Officials asked to further improve the results to reach 95 per cent

title: BJP slams Pinarayi for supporting Iran, calls it ‘appeasement politics’
content: BJP State president Rajeev Chandrasekhar asks CM why he is silent on attacks by Iran on Gulf Cooperation Council countries, where large number of Keralites reside

title: Sobha Surendran launches campaign in Palakkad; district BJP chief’s absence fuels speculation
content: Absence of BJP East district president Prasanth Sivan from Ms. Surendran’s programmes sparked speculation about growing dissent within the party and his dissatisfaction over being denied the Palakkad seat

title: Work on Sandbox’s mini-tech park to begin on March 9
content: The tech park aims to provide modern office facilities for technology companies, global enterprises, start-ups, and global capability centres

title: Visakhapatnam metro rail project to enhance traffic flow with new flyovers
content: NHAI proposed to construct 12 flyovers along NH-16 passing through Visakhapatnam to ensure smooth flow of freight and passenger traffic, says A.P. Metro Rail Corporation MD

title: A Class 10 student punches lab assistant at Veeravalli ZPHS in Krishna district, video goes viral
content: The DEO conducts inquiry, student counselled while action proposed against librarian who filmed and released clip on social media

title: Kerala plantation workers’ daily pay increased by ₹48
content: Labourers in the rubber, tea, coffee, and cardamom sectors covered. As interim relief, workers will receive a one-time payment of ₹1,000

title: Nitish Kumar’s Rajya Sabha move may lead to ‘ideological takeover’ in Bihar politics: CPI(ML) Liberation
content: CPI(ML) Liberation general secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya claims development reflects the growing dominance of the BJP over its ally Janata Dal (United)

title: LDF seeks to punch holes in Rahul Gandhi’s election promises
content: Transport Minister K.B. Ganesh Kumar says Rahul’s election promise of free travel for women on KSRTC will ruin the financially struggling corporation

title: 18 MedTech innovations under pilot testing in A.P. government hospitals
content: Technologies ranging from AI-powered diagnostics to tele-medicine and non-invasive tools being tested in hospitals across the State as part of MedTech Innovation Challenge launched by the government

title: Pawan Kalyan, Lokesh salute women on International Women’s Day
content: True progress of a nation is measured by the dignity, safety and empowerment of its women, says the Deputy Chief Minister, while HRD Minister says that respecting women is a moral value that every one should demonstrate in practice

title: IUML protests move to allot Thrikkarippur seat to Kerala Congress
content: The protest grew stronger after indications emerged that the Kerala Congress was being given priority in the seat-sharing talks despite having only a limited presence in the constituency

title: India earned great respect by remaining neutral between global powers, says Kharge
content: The Leader of Opposition in the Rajya Sabha says PM Modi is harming India’s global standing through his foreign policy decisions

title: Kerala’s diaspora engagement in higher education draws interest from Sri Lanka
content: In a virtual meeting, Sri Lankan officials discuss Scholar Connect initiative with KSHEC representatives, explore possibilities of integrating it with ‘Praja Shakthi’, a flagship development programme launched by the Anura Kumara Dissanayake-led administration.

title: Karnataka Budget 2026-27: Share of Central funds decreasing by the year adds to fiscal pressure
content: Medium Term Fiscal Plan shows the share of Central transfers in the State’s resource envelope reduced from 32.1% in 2017-18 to 25.1% in 2026-27, a drop of 21.8%

title: Tribal groups seek Paniya representation in seats reserved for ST in Wayanad
content: In a detailed submission to leaders of UDF and LDF, the organisations urged the nomination of C. Manikandan, an MBA graduate and social activist from the Paniya community, calling his candidature a step towards “reparative social justice” for a marginalised Adivasi group in the State

title: NDA striving for advancement of women, says Andhra Pradesh CM Chandrababu Naidu
content: So far, 1,12,686 women have established MSMEs, he says, and promises to help in marketing products made by DWCRA and MEPMA groups under ‘Swayam’ brand through various platforms; SERP and MEPMA sign MoUs with various organisation to support SHGs in different fields

title: Three youths killed after speeding car rams truck
content: According to the police, the accident took place at around 1.15 a.m. when the car was heading towards Harohalli from Bengaluru

title: Former Puducherry MP files PIL for early local body polls in U.T.
content: Madras High Court orders notices, returnable by March 27, to Puducherry government and State Election Commission

title: KCBC lashes out at liquor policy of LDF govt. in circular read across churches
content: Circular comes in the wake of a State government decision in February, 2026 to extend the operating hours of bars and beer and wine parlours by two hours

title: Workshop on modern drug design concludes in Mysuru
content: Around 62 delegates, including faculty members, research scholars, and postgraduate students from various institutions across the south zone, took part in the workshop

title: State Information Commission slaps ₹1 lakh penalty on BDA official for failing to provide information under RTI
content: The commission has chosen to levy penalty on the erring officials taking serious view that failure to provide information under RTI on such a mandatory process will go against the spirit of the legislation

title: West Asia conflict hits premium airport cab services in Bengaluru
content: Operators say the decline is linked to disruptions in international travel and a fall in visits by foreign investors and business delegates to the city.

title: Samiti welcomes steps against drug abuse and digital addiction
content: ‘Substance abuse and excessive digital use are increasingly affecting youth, students and families in the State’

title: Chittoor Collector calls for steps to improve gender ratio and curb child marriages in Rayalaseema
content: Nearly 12 to 15% of marriages involve minors, says Sumit Kumar at International Women’s Day programme in Chittoor

title: Andhra Pradesh Mahila Commission to hold ‘Jan Sunwai’ from March 9 to 13
content: Programme to be held in Nellore, Guntur, Vijayawada, Rajamahendravaram and Visakhapatnam to receive women’s petitions and address their grievances, says the Commission Chairperson

title: Special drive against illegal Bangladeshi immigrants reveal a suspected human trafficking network
content: Several Bangladeshi nationals were brought to Bengaluru illegally about four years ago after being promised decent jobs and good salaries

title: One lakh lawyers eligible to vote in Bar Council of Tamil Nadu and Puducherry elections this year
content: The whopping number is in stark contrast to just 37,474 lawyers who had voted in the 2018 election when around 50,000 lawyers were found eligible to vote

title: EOW charges 7 for misusing school building funds in Kupwara
content: During the investigation, it surfaced that although an amount of ₹5,73,592 had been released through various cheques by the then zonal education officer Langate, officials said

title: BJP leaders criticise Siddaramaiah’s Budget for reducing focus on development
content: It leaves the State burdened with lakhs of crores of debts and is full of thousands of false promises, says Abhay Patil

title: DCRE police stations to function on government holidays
content: Directorate of Civil Rights Enforcement police stations were established across the State as special stations to handle cases related to caste-based abuse and atrocities against SC/ST persons.

title: Kumbhabhishekam concludes on a grand note at Kanaka Durga temple in Vijayawada
content: Pontiff of Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham Shankara Vijayendra Saraswati performs the sacred consecration on the Vimana Gopuram as hundreds of devotees witness the rituals atop Indrakeeladri

title: Jagan extols role women play in building a strong society
content: The progress of families and overall development of State are closely linked to economic empowerment of women, says the YSRCP chief

title: Scheduled Caste youth dies in police custody in Sivaganga, family alleges torture
content: According to sources, the Manamadurai police had arrested Akash, 26, and his acquaintance Guna, both from Manamadurai, two days ago for allegedly attacking two men with sharp weapons and causing injuries

title: Himachal to bring law ensuring 25% reservation for women in Class-III recruitment, says CM Sukhu
content: Says a special recruitment drive for women sub-inspectors will be undertaken in the Police Department.

title: SUV catches fire after hit-and-run accident in Bengaluru; woman injured
content: After the collision, the SUV driver allegedly continued to drive, dragging the motorcycle along the road. During the process, petrol leaked from the bike and the friction is suspected to have triggered a fire in the car.

title: Need to adopt holistic, inclusive approach towards women's issues: Pradhan
content: “We cannot work on women’s issues in isolation. There is need to adopt a holistic and inclusive approach towards them,” Dharmendra Pradhan said

title: Cruise ship with 619 passengers calls at New Mangalore Port
content: It was the third cruise vessel of the current cruise season. The ship’s itinerary covered Colombo, Kochi, Mangaluru, Goa and Mumbai.

title: Second part of Budget Session all set for stormy start
content: The Opposition, which will be meeting on March 9 for a strategy session at Mallikarjun Kharge’s office, is hoping to press its point, at least in public discourse, as the numbers show that the motion will be defeated

title: Mega ex-servicemen rally in Assam focuses on civilian careers
content: Eight government agencies and four private sector groups provide guidance on employment opportunities, and 4,000 veterans and war widows attend an event organised by Gajraj Corps; guidance provided on jobs and business opportunities

title: Last-minute NFL free agency nuggets: What we're hearing on Eagles plans, top RBs and WRs
content: What are we hearing as free agency nears? We give you the latest buzz on top players and aggressive teams.

title: Murray? Tua?? Geno?! Taking a look at who could be the Jets' starting QB in 2026
content: New York has plenty of options to revamp its quarterbacks room through free agency, trading and the draft.

title: Skubal mulls WBC plan, may pitch again for U.S.
content: Tarik Skubal says he is considering pitching again for the United States at the World Baseball Classic, a potential change in plans for the Tigers' ace.

title: White: Jones never in mind for White House card
content: UFC legends Jon Jones and Conor McGregor were both left off the promotion's planned White House card, with Dana White saying Jones was "never ever" in his mind for a fight.

title: Pack bring back versatile Rhyan on 3-year deal
content: The Packers have agreed to bring back Sean Rhyan on a three-year, $33 million deal, his agents told ESPN.

title: Japan rallies past Australia, clinches WBC pool
content: Defending champion Japan rallied from 1-0 down to beat Australia 4-3 in the World Baseball Classic on Sunday behind a two-run homer from Masataka Yoshida with two outs in the seventh inning.

title: Messi paid $70M-$80M per year, Miami owner says
content: Inter Miami co-owner Jorge Mas revealed that captain Lionel Messi is paid between $70 million and $80 million a year, including the player's ownership shares.

title: Hurley tossed, fined as UConn falls to Marquette
content: UConn coach Danny Hurley was ejected in the final second as Marquette hung on for a 68-62 victory that prevented the fourth-ranked Huskies from winning a share of the Big East regular-season title and hurt their hopes for a 1-seed in the NCAA tournament.

title: The pride and power of Iowa State basketball's Audi Crooks
content: Audi Crooks hears the haters. But Iowa State's star inherited resilience from her mom and joy from her dad. Her high-scoring game is the ultimate counterpoint.

title: Women's Bracketology: No. 1 seeds set, but bubble is toughest in years
content: Texas and South Carolina are locks for 1-seeds. TCU replaces Michigan as the final 2-seed. The bubble? Wide open.

title: NHL playoff watch: Who will win the Pacific Division title?
content: It's Golden Knights-Oilers at 9:30 p.m. on ESPN. Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: Who aced the trade deadline? Six winners, six losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: 'LOL THATS IT?': MMA fighters react to UFC White House card
content: The polarizing fight card generated online comments from stars such as Conor McGregor and Sean O'Malley.

title: Predicting the USWNT's starting lineup for next year's World Cup
content: With the 2027 Women's World Cup closer than you think, we look at the USWNT's roster and forecast the 11 players Emma Hayes will count on for the tournament.

title: Wrexham dreaming of the Premier League? Chelsea give them a reality check
content: Wrexham are looking to get promoted to the Premier League, but their FA Cup loss to Chelsea gives them a reminder of what they will expect should they get promoted.

title: F1's new rules create 'Mario Kart' racing in Australia season opener
content: F1 preseason has been dominated by criticism of its new rules, and while Australia's season opener boasted lots of passing, the critiques didn't stop.

title: How Duke's Krzyzewskiville ordering test decides who gets the best 'Cameron Crazies' seats
content: There aren't many tests at Duke that will produce a median score among takers of 43.2%. But Krzyzewskiville's annual "ordering test" isn't just any test.

title: Solak: Why Crosby fits perfectly with the Ravens' D -- and their Super Bowl window
content: Baltimore had to give up two first-round picks to get him, but Crosby can dominate in the Ravens' defense.

title: Reid's updated NFL draft QB Hot Board: Strengths and weaknesses for the top 12 passers
content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

title: Bitterness turns into celebration as Puerto Rico's WBC journey begins
content: Insurance controversy rocked the roster, but the home team in Pool A of the World Baseball Classic is writing its next chapter.

title: Predictions: Will Japan repeat or is United States the team to beat?
content: Our experts make their picks for every round of the 2026 WBC -- from pool play through the championship game.

title: The Alex Eala Effect: How a 20-year-old phenom has taken the tennis world by storm
content: Filipina tennis prodigy Alex Eala has drawn enormous crowds everywhere she goes. On Sunday, she will take on Coco Gauff at Indian Wells.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid join Arsenal in race for Tonali
content: Real Madrid have taken an interest in Newcastle's Sandro Tonali, while Bernardo Silva is set to leave Man City. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Steve Emt's incredible story: From UConn basketball to three-time Paralympian
content: Former UConn basketball player Steve Emt was paralyzed in a car accident at 25. Then he discovered his immense skill in Paralympic curling.

title: Man Arrested For Drowning 3 Minor Daughters In Lake In Telangana: Cops
content: An auto driver in the Kamareddy district of Telangana was arrested for allegedly killing his three minor daughters by drowning them in a lake, police said on Sunday.

title: "Why Governor Resigned," Asks Mamata Banerjee. "Confidential," Says CV Bose
content: Former West Bengal Governor CV Bose, whose sudden resignation has raised eyebrows, has now said the reason is "confidential" but he will share it at an "appropriate" time.

title: First Came The Inferno, Then Darkness: Tehran Blackened After Oil Depots Hit
content: Residents of Tehran woke up on Sunday expecting daylight. Instead, they found the city still shrouded in darkness.

title: 3-Year-Old Gets AI-Based Cochlear Implant At Hospital In Rajasthan
content: In a first for the government hospitals in the state, an artificial intelligence-based smart cochlear implant has been implanted in the ear of a three-year-old girl at the Rajasthan University of Health Sciences here.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Body Chooses Next Supreme Leader, Name Not Announced Yet, Says Report
content: The war in the Middle East was triggered after the US and Israel attacked Iran last Saturday, killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: "Chacha Idhar Dhyaan De": Viral Video Claims Iran Missile Onslaught On Qatar
content: Nine days into the American and Israeli military campaign against Iran, Tehran's retaliatory strikes are showing no signs of letting up, and a video allegedly filmed in Qatar shows the chaos of living close to US military bases as strikes continue.

title: "Enemy's Misconceptions": Iran President's U-Turn After Apology To Neighbours
content: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian appeared to backtrack from his conciliatory remark towards his Gulf neighbours.

title: Married Woman's Body Found Hanging From Tree Near Canal In UP: Cops
content: A 26-year-old woman's body was found hanging from a tree alongside a canal here, half-submerged in water, on Sunday, police said.

title: Manipur To Give Rs 10,000 Each To 3.5 Lakh Women Affected By 2023 Violence
content: The Manipur government will make a budgetary provision of Rs 350 crore to support around 3.5 lakh women, Chief Minister Y Khemchand Singh said on Sunday.

title: Darknet Drug Network 'Team Kalki' Busted, Sent Over 1,000 Parcels Across India
content: The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) has dismantled a darknet-based drug trafficking network that, by its own operational count, sent more than 1,000 narcotics parcels to buyers across India - from Delhi to Kerala - between January 2025 and this month.

title: Route To Lachen, Gateway To Gurudongmar Lake, Reopens In Sikkim
content: The road to Lachen in North Sikkim, which had remained closed following the June 2025 disaster, has now been reopened and tourist permits are being issued again.

title: "Insulted President On Women's Day": PM Modi's Sharp Attack On Trinamool
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi continued his attack on Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee over the issues around the visit of President Droupadi Murmu.

title: The Significance Of Mamata Banerjee Returning To Kolkata's Protest Epicentre
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee is facing her toughest elections yet, with the principal opposition party--the BJP--as the primary challenger in West Bengal.

title: Video: Apocalyptic Inferno Lights Up Iranian Capital As Massive Fire Rages
content: Massive blasts and towering plumes of smoke lit up Tehran after Israeli airstrikes targeted five key fuel depots in the Iranian capital. Four people, including two tanker drivers, were killed.

title: AAP Fulfils Promise To Punjab Women 4 Years After Polls, Congress Protests
content: Four years after the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) came to power in Punjab, and less than a year before the state goes to polls again, the Bhagwant Mann government has fulfilled its poll promise of giving a monthly assistance of Rs 1,000 to women

title: Gautam Adani Pays Tribute To Women In His Family On Women's Day
content: Gautam Adani, chairman of the Adani Group, stated on Sunday that women in his family have played a decisive role in shaping his outlook on life, responsibility and nation-building.

title: Iran's Shahed Missions Trigger Acceleration In India's Strike Drone Projects
content: The large-scale use of the Shahed series of low-cost drones by Iran in its war with Israel has added urgency and accelerated India's long-range low-cost strike drone projects.

title: Video: UP School Headmistress Takes Massages From Students, Suspended
content: A primary school headmistress in Uttar Pradesh's Chitrakoot has been suspended after a video showing girl students massaging her during school hours went viral on social media.

title: 1 Dead After Class 9 Boy Attacks Students With Iron Rod In Karnataka School Hostel
content: While students in a Karnataka school hostel were asleep, one of the students picked up an iron rod and went on a rampage on Saturday, killing one and injuring several others.

title: Police book Manjeri MLA’s son for attacking shop owner
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: How Middle East crisis is making life difficult in Bangladesh and Pakistan
content: Escalating West Asian conflict triggers economic fallout in South Asia, with Bangladesh implementing fuel rationing amid panic buying and Pakistan facing shortages and price hikes. Border trade disruptions in Balochistan exacerbate the crisis, impacting essential commodity supplies and livelihoods.

title: Sanju Samson scripts history, breaks multiple records in T20 World Cup final
content: Sanju Samson's explosive 89 in the T20 World Cup final powered India to a historic feat, with the top three batters scoring 50+. Samson also set a new record for most sixes in a single tournament with 21. He joins an elite group with semi-final and final fifties, and matches consecutive 50+ scores, solidifying his status as a tournament standout.

title: 'Why? Why? Why?' Mamata questions timing of President venue row before Bengal polls
content: Speaking from the central Kolkata sit-in platform, Banerjee responded to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s criticism earlier in the day, in which he accused the TMC government of “insulting” a woman tribal leader and the President of the country. In reply, Banerjee displayed an enlarged photograph showing the Prime Minister seated beside veteran BJP leader LK Advani while the President stood nearby.

title: Amazon sends letter to FCC saying: Reject application of Elon Musk's Spacex for Space data centers
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and orbital parameters. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially hoarding orbital resources.

title: Oli’s tabla gift, 'riyaaz' advice to Balen 4 yrs ago turns into Nepal poll’s sharpest irony
content: Former PM KP Sharma Oli, who once gifted Kathmandu Mayor Balendra Shah a tabla, congratulated him on his recent electoral victory. Their relationship, initially cordial, soured significantly, culminating in a bitter election contest where Shah defeated Oli by a wide margin, highlighting a stark political shift.

title: Groups representing 700,000 Amazon, Google, Microsoft workers urge firms to ‘do right’
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that could compromise AI safety guardrails.  The open letter, from 'No Tech For Apartheid,' urges leaders to refuse demands that would allow AI for mass surveillance and autonomous weaponry, citing concerns over complicity in violence and repression.

title: T20 World Cup Final: India attempt to defend crown, script record third T20 title in Ahmedabad
content: India stands on the brink of a historic third T20 World Cup title, facing New Zealand in Sunday's final at the Narendra Modi Stadium. Aiming to become the first team to defend their title, Suryakumar Yadav's men carry the weight of expectation, seeking redemption on a ground that has previously seen their World Cup final defeats.

title: ‘Apocalyptic’ scenes in Tehran: Massive inferno erupts after explosions – video
content: Israeli airstrikes targeted multiple fuel storage facilities in Tehran and Karaj, causing large fires and explosions. The Israeli military confirmed the strikes, stating the facilities supported Iran's armed forces. In retaliation, Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched a new wave of attacks against Israel, which also reportedly struck ballistic missile sites. At least four people were killed in the strikes.

title: Salesforce CEO on layoffs at Jack Dorsey’s Block: Company has its own issues
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff dismissed Block's massive layoffs as company-specific issues, not an AI-driven jobs apocalypse. He argued AI boosts productivity without wholesale cuts, citing Salesforce's growth. Skeptics, however, suggest Block's cuts are due to pandemic-era bloat rather than AI advancements.

title: Abhishek, Samson tear into NZ; break 17-year record in T20 World Cup final
content: India's opening duo, Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson, powered to a record 98-run partnership in the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand. Sharma's rapid fifty set the tone, continuing Samson's strong tournament form. This explosive start provided India with a significant advantage as they aim for a historic third title on home soil.

title: 'Will not sign other bills until Save America Act is passed': Donald Trump
content: President Trump declared he will not sign any further legislation until Congress passes the Save America Act, deeming it a top priority. He urged lawmakers to expedite its passage, even suggesting a filibuster.

title: Dhoni and Rohit bring out T20 WC trophy, Ahmedabad crowd roars - Watch
content: Dhoni had led India to the first T20 World Cup title in 2007, while Rohit captained the side to their second title in the 2024 edition.

title: The little-known Jaipur connection of the T20 World Cup trophy
content: Jaipur-based designer Amit Pabuwal said he made the T20 World Cup trophy after the initial design concept was prepared by Australia’s Minale Bryce.

title: 'Rules of this game ... ': Ex-Bengal governor's cryptic remark on his resignation
content: Speaking to reporters at the Kolkata airport, Bose told news agency PTI that he, however, would return to Bengal to cast his vote. Bose, a native of Kerala, had shifted his voting base to Bengal barely a week before tendering resignation from his office. Bose avoided a direct response when asked ​to comment on the angst expressed by President Droupadi Murmu on alleged protocol violations and tribal deprivation in the state.

title: Rs 1 shoe offer sparks chaos: Massive crowd gathers at shop, police resort to lathicharge
content: A footwear shop's promotional offer of premium shoes for just Rs 1 triggered chaos in Kozhikode on Sunday morning. Hundreds of people, some arriving as early as 2 am, gathered for the offer, leading to a stampede-like situation and traffic disruption. Police resorted to a lathicharge to disperse the unruly crowd, and shop owners were taken into custody.

title: ‘Hasn’t learned from his dismissals’: Gavaskar wants Abhishek dropped for final
content: Former captain Sunil Gavaskar proposes alterations to India's lineup for the T20 World Cup final. He suggests dropping Abhishek Sharma due to consistent batting struggles. Rinku Singh could replace him, with Ishan Kishan and Sanju Samson opening. Gavaskar also considers bringing in Kuldeep Yadav for Varun Chakravarthy, citing the latter's recent struggles and Ahmedabad's pitch conditions.

title: Happy International Women's Day: 75+ messages, greetings, wishes and quotes
content: International Women's Day prompts reflection on the vital roles women play, often quietly.  The article highlights the significance of acknowledging their strength, effort, and presence.  It offers a collection of messages, wishes, and quotes to help express appreciation for the women who enrich our lives, encouraging a pause to recognize their impact.

title: MH370 still missing: Latest search after scanning 15,000 sq km ends with no clues to 239 people
content: The latest search for Malaysia Airlines flight MH370, marking its 12th anniversary, has concluded without any trace of the missing aircraft. Despite extensive seabed scanning by Ocean Infinity, the plane and its 239 occupants remain lost. Families of Chinese passengers expressed deep frustration over a lack of communication and psychological support, highlighting the enduring mystery and emotional toll.

title: India’s first Ring Metro: PM Modi inaugurates Majlis Park–Maujpur–Babarpur Pink Line
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated two new metro corridors in Delhi, completing India's first fully operational Ring Metro. The 12.3 km Pink Line extension and the 9.9 km Magenta Line section aim to enhance connectivity, reduce travel times, and ease congestion and pollution in the capital.

title: 'Kia Ora Aotearoa': Outnumbered in Ahmedabad, NZ's Santner calls home before final
content: New Zealand captain Mitch Santner has asked fans to wake up early for the T20 World Cup final against India. The match starts at 2:30 am in New Zealand. Santner acknowledged the team will be outnumbered by Indian supporters. He encouraged fans to set alarms and support the Blackcaps. The team hopes to return home with the trophy.

title: T20 World Cup 2026: Ahmedabad, the final, and the ghost of November 19 past
content: Silence and crowd noise were back in discussion ahead of Sunday's final but India captain Suryakumar Yadav chose to downplay the chatter. He acknowledged the challenges, pressure and excitement a final brings, and his message to the team was simple: be courageous.

title: Parliament’s Budget Session set to resume: What to expect as Centre–opposition showdown looms
content: Opposition's no-confidence motion against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla is set for debate, largely symbolic ahead of assembly polls. While unlikely to succeed, the move aims to project INDIA bloc unity and will likely see broader issues like US tariffs and Epstein files raised. The government is prepared to defend Birla and counter with its own points.

title: Iran picks next Supreme Leader after Khamenei, name yet to be announced
content: Iran's Assembly of Experts has selected a successor for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following his death in joint US-Israeli airstrikes. Members confirmed a majority-approved candidate has been determined, though the name remains undisclosed. This marks a critical transition for Iran, preparing for its second leadership change since the 1979 revolution amid heightened regional tensions.

title: 'I can feel pulse of the player': Rohit reveals reason behind Samson pep talk
content: Sanju Samson has become India's T20 World Cup 2026 hero with match-winning knocks against the West Indies and England.  Captain Rohit Sharma's pre-tournament encouragement, recalling his own experiences, proved vital.  Samson, who missed the 2024 edition without playing, has seized his chance, now a leading Player of the Tournament contender with exceptional form and two Player of the Match awards.

title: Opium crop found on farmland linked to BJP leader in Chhattisgarh
content: Police in Durg district uncovered nearly 1.5 acres of illegal opium cultivation, strategically concealed within a jowar crop. The land is linked to a local BJP Kisan Morcha leader, who has been suspended by the party. Former CM Bhupesh Baghel visited the site, alleging political connections and demanding answers from the ruling BJP.

title: Hormuz supply shock: India diversifies oil basket as Middle East conflict drags on
content: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions, India is rerouting crude oil supplies to secure uninterrupted fuel imports. Refiners are increasing sourcing from the US, West Africa, and Russia, while holding planned maintenance to build reserves. This diversification aims to mitigate risks associated with the Strait of Hormuz, ensuring domestic fuel needs are met despite potential cost increases.

title: Man who accused Hindu seer of sexual harassment attacked in UP
content: Ahutosh Brahmachari, who filed a sexual harassment case against Swami Avimukteshwaranand, was allegedly attacked with a sharp weapon on the Rewa Express near Sirathu railway station. He sustained injuries to his nose and forearm after locking himself in a train toilet. Senior GRP authorities have launched a detailed probe into the incident.

title: 'Changing face of Mid East': Netanyahu thanks Trump for strikes on Iran
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced near-total dominance over Iranian airspace following coordinated strikes with the US, dubbed 'Operation Rising Lion.' He stated the campaign, supported by former President Trump, aimed to neutralize an immediate threat from Iran's nuclear ambitions and missile program, emphasizing a strategic shift in the region post-October 7th.

title: From one World Cup winner to another: India women back men's team ahead of final
content: Members of the India women's team and head coach Amol Muzumdar have sent their best wishes to the men's team ahead of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. Following their own recent World Cup victory, the women's team expressed their support through a video, encouraging the men to bring the trophy home.

title: 'Won't leave Trump alone': Khamenei aide warns; US president shrugs off threat
content: Iran's top security official has vowed retaliation against US President Trump for the killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei and over 1,000 Iranians.  Tehran launched missile strikes on Israel and US bases following the joint US-Israeli attacks. Trump dismissed the threats, calling for Iran's unconditional surrender, while Iran warned regional nations against aiding US operations.

title: 'Arrogance of power will soon be shattered': PM Modi attacks Mamata over Murmu's event row
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi strongly criticized West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and the Trinamool Congress for allegedly mismanaging and boycotting an event attended by President Droupadi Murmu. Modi termed the actions an insult to the President, the Constitution, and democratic traditions, warning that the people of West Bengal would not forgive this perceived disrespect towards a tribal woman leader.

title: SA players slam ICC after England fly home while they remain stranded
content: Players from South Africa national cricket team and West Indies national cricket team criticised travel arrangements after their exit from the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, questioning why England national cricket team flew home earlier. Quinton de Kock and David Miller voiced frustration, while Daren Sammy and Michael Vaughan also questioned the situation.

title: Nitish Kumar's son Nishant enters politics, joins JD(U)
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar's son, Nishant Kumar, has formally joined the JD(U). This development follows Nitish Kumar's decision to step down as CM, sparking speculation about his successor and potentially ushering in a new political era in Bihar. Nitish Kumar himself announced his intention to contest the Rajya Sabha elections.

title: ‘A strong fist doesn't mean strong reason’: China warns against regime change in Iran
content: China's foreign minister Wang Yi cautioned against externally driven regime change in Iran, stating such efforts would lack public backing and escalate regional instability. He emphasized respecting Iran's sovereignty and urged an immediate end to hostilities, advocating for diplomatic solutions and independent regional self-determination. Beijing is ready to help restore order and peace.

title: 250 million barrel buffer: How much oil does India have and how long will it last
content: India's energy security is bolstered by over 250 million barrels of oil reserves, sufficient for nearly two months. Diversified crude imports from 40 countries and a reduced reliance on the Strait of Hormuz mitigate geopolitical risks. India continues to import Russian oil, complying with G7 price caps, while domestic ethanol blending and expanded refining capacity further strengthen its energy independence.

title: PM Modi inaugurates two new Delhi Metro corridors: Key routes and how they will help commuters
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has inaugurated and laid the foundation stone for infrastructure projects worth Rs 33,500 crore in Delhi. This includes two new Delhi Metro corridors, totaling over Rs 18,300 crore, aimed at enhancing public transport and connectivity. The event also features the foundation stone laying for three new metro routes under Phase V-A and GPRA redevelopment projects.

title: Punjab’s most-wanted gangster Amritpal Singh detained in Europe on Interpol red corner notice
content: Amritpal Singh, a key figure in the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria gang, has been detained on the Moldova border following an Interpol red corner notice. Punjab Police's newly formed Overseas Fugitive Tracking and Extradition Cell played a crucial role in his apprehension. Legal and diplomatic processes are now underway for his extradition to India to face serious charges.

title: As a 'mysterious' illness sidelines Kristaps Porzingis, the Warriors reckon with the future
content: KRISTAPS PORZINGIS ' DEBUT' with the Golden State Warriors on febr. 19 went about as one would expect from a player who'd been sidelined more than a month . he poured in two post-up jumpers over a mismatch, drilled a 30-footer and volleyball spiked a Nikola Vucevic floater . the Warriors won the 9 minutes, 48 seconds that Porzingis played by 15 points .

title: Rahm calls LIV win 'weight off my shoulders'
content: HONG KONG --  Jon Rahm  won the LIV Golf Hong Kong event Sunday for his first individual title since 2024. Rahm shot 6 under in the final round and finished 23 under for the tournament, three shots ahead of runner-up  Thomas Detry . The Spaniard hadn't won a title since LIV Golf Chicago in 2024. "I've been very ecstatic for wins in the past. This one just feels like a big weight off my shoulders," he said. Back-to-back defending LIV Golf champion Rahm was tied with Detry and  Harold Varner III  at 17 under going into the final round at Hong Kong Golf Club at Fanling.  Thomas Pieters  finished third at 19 under. "I played really good all day," Rahm said. "It was incredible. But I just tried to stay very patient and committed to each shot, knowing that I was doing everything right and things were going to happen." Dustin Johnson 's 4Aces GC took the team prize.
